title: NOC 72406 – Elevator constructors and mechanics | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Elevator Constructors and Mechanics (NOC 72406) are the high-tech specialists ensuring the vertical mobility of Canada’s rapidly growing urban centers. As the country builds taller to solve the housing crisis, these professionals are in high demand to install and maintain the complex systems found in high-rise residential and commercial towers. Operating at the TEER 2 level, the role has evolved into a sophisticated blend of mechanical engineering, industrial electricity, and solid-state electronics. In 2026, top mechanics are increasingly specializing in Smart Elevator technologies, including AI-driven destination dispatch systems and high-speed energy-recovery drives.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Technical Assembly: Reading and interpreting blueprints to determine the layout of system components and performing preparatory steel work, wiring, and piping.
System Installation: Installing elevators, escalators, and moving walkways according to specifications, and connecting car frames to counterweights with specialized cables.
Electronic Control: Installing and wiring sophisticated electric and electronic control systems and safety devices.
Diagnostics & Repair: Troubleshooting electrical or mechanical failures and disassembling defective units to repair or replace worn components like valves, seals, and brake linings.
Safety & Compliance: Carrying out strict preventative maintenance programs to ensure public safety and compliance with the 2026 National Safety Codes for Elevating Devices.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a four- to five-year apprenticeship program (approximately 8,000 hours) is usually required to be eligible for trade certification.
Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Alberta and Quebec. It is voluntary but highly recommended in all other provinces.
Regulatory Licensing: In Ontario, a certificate from the Technical Standards and Safety Authority (TSSA) is required to legally practice as an Elevating Devices Mechanic (EDM).
Skills: Proficiency in mechanical print reading, rigging and hoisting, and industrial electricity for elevating devices.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, specifically driven by the "High-Rise Boom" in major metropolitan areas:

Ontario (Toronto/GTA): Highest Demand. With over 1,720 people employed in this sector in Toronto alone, Ontario remains the primary hub for this trade. In March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for skilled trades, with regional scores in Central Ontario starting at 55, reflecting a steady need for elevator modernization.

Alberta: Strong demand / Compulsory Trade. Alberta is one of the few provinces where trade certification is compulsory. Median wages in Alberta are among the highest in Canada, reaching $51.00/hr to support the growing skylines of Calgary and Edmonton.

British Columbia: High Priority. This NOC is currently on the BC PNP Priority Occupations List for construction. Candidates with a valid trade certificate from SkilledTradesBC benefit from targeted draws and faster PR processing.

National Trend: Over 95% of the workforce is male, and nearly 90% of workers receive comprehensive non-wage benefits. Approximately 74% of elevator mechanics work all year round—significantly higher than other construction-related trades.
Why Work in Canada as an Elevator Mechanic?
Lead the Vertical Revolution: Gain hands-on experience with 2026-grade technologies, including Twin Elevator systems, destination control, and contactless sensor arrays.
Unmatched Financial Stability: As a TEER 2 trade, your median hourly wage in Canada is $43.50/hr, with high-earners in major cities like Toronto and Calgary reaching upwards of $68.00/hr.

Recession-Resistant Skills: While new construction may fluctuate, the mandatory safety inspections and maintenance of the existing stock of hundreds of thousands of elevators provide constant, stable work.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC remains a core target for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category, which now requires 12 months of qualifying experience.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time work experience within the last 3 years to receive an ITA at lower CRS scores.

OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72406 under the Skilled Trades and In-Demand Skills streams. This is a high-success route for those with an Ontario job offer.
BC PNP Construction Stream: As a Priority Occupation, elevator mechanics benefit from targeted draws and faster processing if they have a valid BC job offer.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Elevator mechanics are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
